doc_title: A Neural Network Architecture Combining Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) and Support Vector Machine (SVM) for Intrusion Detection in Network Traffic Data

items.fact: Google TensorFlow[1] was used to implement the neural network models
items.evidence_span: Google TensorFlow[1] was used to implement the neural network models in this study

items.fact: The 2013 Kyoto University honeypot systems' network traffic data[20] was used in this study
entities: 2013 Kyoto University honeypot systems' network traffic data
items.evidence_span: The 2013 Kyoto University honeypot systems' network traffic data[20] was used in this study

items.fact: Song, Takakura, & Okabe (2006)[20] might be pivotal in a more effective investigation on intrusion detection
items.evidence_span: according to Song, Takakura, \& Okabe (2006)[20], might be pivotal in a more effective investigation on intrusion detection

items.fact: Scikit-learn[19] was used for the data standardization in this study
items.evidence_span: the StandardScaler().fit_transform() function of Scikit-learn[19] was used for the data standardization in this study

items.fact: pandas[16] was used for decile binning
items.evidence_span: This process was done using the qcut () function of pandas[16]

items.fact: Alalshekmubarak & Smith (2013)[2] and Tang (2013)[22] proposed to use SVM as the classifier in a neural network architecture
items.evidence_span: Similar to the work of Alalshekmubarak \& Smith (2013)[2] and Tang (2013)[22], the present paper proposes to use SVM as the classifier in a neural network architecture

items.fact: GRU[3] was used for the gating mechanism
items.evidence_span: the parameters are learned through the gating mechanism of GRU[3]

items.fact: Adam[12] optimizer was used for loss minimization
